prompt: I maintain a Python library that currently uses `asyncio.create_task()`, `asyncio.Queue()`, and `asyncio.wait_for()` directly. One part of the library also calls an asyncio-native vendor SDK that spawns its own tasks. I want the core API to run on both asyncio and Trio without lying about portability. Show the right redesign direction under AnyIO and call out the places where native backend details still matter.
assertions: It recommends AnyIO task groups instead of raw `asyncio.create_task()` for owned child work.
It recommends memory object streams over raw `asyncio.Queue()` for new portable designs.
It replaces `asyncio.wait_for()` with AnyIO timeout/cancel-scope guidance rather than leaving it untouched.
It explicitly states that tasks spawned by native backend libraries on backends other than Trio are not subject to AnyIO's cancellation rules.
It refuses to call the library fully portable while it still depends on an asyncio-native SDK that spawns its own tasks.
It requires verification on both asyncio and Trio rather than a single-backend test run.
